name: Michael Lee
tagline: M.S. Analytics · Georgia Tech  ·  B.S. Information Science · UMD
bio: I build things end-to-end, from data pipelines to deployed SaaS products. I work best at the intersection of engineering, data, and business strategy. Background in USAF with a focus on data, federal, and defense roles.

projects.name: ReviewStar
projects.problem: Job seekers practice behavioral interviews without structured feedback and generic advice doesn't help them improve at the STAR level.
projects.evidence: Built a full-stack SaaS with a FastAPI backend, Next.js frontend, Supabase auth and PostgreSQL, Google OAuth, mic or text input, and LLaMA 3.3 70B via Groq for per-section STAR scoring and answer rewrites. Implemented three-tier usage limits, Stripe live subscriptions, and a Pro session history dashboard.
projects.result: Live at reviewstar.cv on AWS EC2 with paying Free and Pro ($9/mo) subscribers.
highlights: Scores each STAR section individually on a 1-10 scale with overall feedback and an answer rewrite in a single Groq API call
Dual rate-limiting: IP-based (20/day) for anonymous users and Supabase-persisted monthly counts for logged-in tiers
Streak bonus system: daily login credits, ad-watch credits, and 7/14/30-day milestones unlock 5/8/15 bonus credits
Practice Session mode queues 5 questions scored independently with a Pro session history dashboard and SVG trend charts
Next.js 16 static export served directly by FastAPI/uvicorn on AWS EC2 Ubuntu with systemd service management
Live Stripe recurring billing at $9/mo with webhook-driven plan upgrades reflected in Supabase profiles table

projects.name: AWS IAM Lifecycle Pipeline
projects.problem: Manual IAM management in enterprise environments creates compliance gaps as stale accounts linger after offboarding and privilege creep goes undetected.
projects.evidence: Automated joiner, mover, and leaver workflows with Terraform-provisioned infra, MFA enforcement via policy condition, least-privilege role assignment, CloudTrail audit logging, IAM Access Analyzer integration, dry-run mode, and full pytest coverage. Users are never deleted; a deprovisioned tag preserves the audit trail for AC-2 compliance.
projects.result: Production-ready pipeline satisfying NIST 800-53 and CMMC Level 2 controls with a full per-change audit trail.
highlights: Three lifecycle phases: 7-step provisioning (joiner), group update (mover), and 7-step removal with key revocation (leaver)
MFA-first enforcement: users cannot perform any AWS action until aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent is true at provisioning
Immutable audit trail: users are never deleted; every action logged to local JSONL, S3 (versioned + encrypted), and CloudTrail
Department-scoped least-privilege groups: engineers, finance, HR, security, and contractors each get distinct IAM policies
Access Analyzer flags dormant accounts (90+ day inactivity) and overly permissive policies on every review run
Dry-run flag logs all planned actions without AWS API calls; CI runs full pipeline in dry-run on every push

projects.name: AI Labor Market Risk Pipeline
projects.problem: No model existed to correlate AI-exposure risk with financial vulnerability across occupations, making it difficult to assess credit risk in AI-disrupted labor markets.
projects.evidence: Built an end-to-end pipeline ingesting from BLS OEWS, Anthropic Economic Index, O*NET, and Census ACS PUMS into Snowflake via Terraform-provisioned AWS S3. Implemented 43 dbt tests across staging, intermediate, and mart layers with a composite score of 0.40 x AI exposure + 0.60 x financial vulnerability.
projects.result: MART_CREDIT_RISK_BY_OCCUPATION ranks every occupation by composite risk score. Top finding: Medical Transcriptionists at 0.847.
highlights: Six-source ETL: BLS OEWS (414K rows), Anthropic Economic Index (756 occupations), O*NET (30K rows), Census ACS PUMS (797K person records)
Composite score: 40% AI automation exposure + 60% financial vulnerability (unemployment, poverty, income instability, work instability)
Financial vulnerability aggregated from ACS microdata with 25% weight each for 4 economic stress indicators
43 dbt tests across staging, intermediate, and mart layers covering nulls, uniqueness, range checks (0-1), and accepted values
15 real-world integration bugs resolved: Anthropic dataset restructure, Census URL migration, BLS 403 errors, Snowflake COPY INTO idempotency, O*NET column renames
Terraform IaC: S3 bucket, IAM role with Snowflake trust policy via external ID, SNS alerting

projects.name: DoD Small Business Contract Pipeline
projects.problem: Federal contract data from USASpending.gov is large, paginated, and raw with no easy way to track DoD small business trends or benchmark vendors.
projects.evidence: Built an end-to-end ETL with paginated REST API ingestion, pandas transforms with NAICS classification and deduplication, PostgreSQL load via SQLAlchemy 2.0, and an interactive Streamlit dashboard with KPI cards, choropleth map, and sidebar filters. Extended to Azure Blob Storage and Azure SQL.
projects.result: Fully functional analytics platform over federal contract data usable for vendor research and competitive landscape analysis.
highlights: Paginated ingestion from USASpending.gov /v2/search/spending_by_award/ with ~1 req/sec rate limiting
6 validation checks: deduplication, negative-amount filtering, date parsing, null tracking, and an 80% pass-rate threshold warning
Pipeline audit table tracks records_in, records_out, pass_rate_pct, and status per run
7 dashboard visualizations: KPI cards, monthly volume, NAICS sector spending, top-15 recipients, award tier donut, choropleth map, raw records table
Sidebar filters for sub-agency, NAICS sector, and award tier applied across all charts with a 5-minute cache TTL
Architecture maps directly to Azure DP-900 scope: Blob Storage, Data Factory, and Azure SQL

projects.name: Job Agent
projects.problem: Manually checking dozens of career pages and USAJobs daily is slow, inconsistent, and easy to miss.
projects.evidence: Built a Playwright scraper targeting Greenhouse, Lever, Workday, and USAJobs with SQLite deduplication, Claude API relevance scoring against a resume and profile summary, and a Gmail HTML email digest all running on a daily cron schedule.
projects.result: Runs every morning and surfaces only relevant opportunities above the score threshold, removing the manual search loop entirely.
highlights: Scrapes 7+ career pages (Greenhouse, Lever, Workday) plus USAJobs API with URL-based SQLite deduplication before scoring
Title keyword pre-filtering before any AI calls: blocks senior/staff/principal/director/VP roles and requires data/analytics/engineering terms
Claude API scores each listing 1-10 against your resume; configurable MIN_RELEVANCE_SCORE threshold (default 6)
Flexible LLM backend: toggle between Claude API and local Ollama (gemma4:26b) with identical scoring interface
HTML email digest sent via Gmail App Password over SMTP TLS 587 on a daily cron at 8am
Dry-run mode scrapes and scores without saving to SQLite or sending email

projects.name: Crypto Spam Tracker
projects.problem: YouTube comment sections for crypto content are flooded with scam promotions with no easy way to monitor which coins are being targeted in real time.
projects.evidence: Built a FastAPI backend with APScheduler polling the YouTube Comments API every 7 minutes, a pattern-matching detector extracting coin mentions, SQLite storage, live price lookup, and a plain-JS dashboard with per-coin filtering.
projects.result: Running monitoring service tracking crypto spam patterns across YouTube with a live dashboard showing comment content, timestamps, and coin-level breakdown.
highlights: 40-pattern spam signature library detecting 18 cryptocurrencies and 17 scam phrases (double your money, Telegram, trading signals, guaranteed profit, 100x returns, etc.)
APScheduler polls YouTube Comments API every 7 minutes with ~1 req/sec polite rate limiting
Three analytics endpoints: /api/comments (filtered spam), /api/stats (coin counts + live prices), /api/timeline/{symbol} (hourly spam vs price)
CoinGecko price integration enables correlation analysis between spam volume and price pump events
Dual-table SQLite schema: spam_comments + price_snapshots, both indexed on captured_at for efficient hourly aggregation
Windows desktop notifications via winotify on new spam detections

projects.name: Game Sales Data Analysis
projects.problem: Global video game sales data from CSV exports had no structure for meaningful querying or trend analysis.
projects.evidence: Designed a normalized MySQL relational database with 6 tables covering games, platforms, publishers, regions, year, and sales; cleaned ~2,000 raw entries; and wrote SQL queries with joins, aggregations, and subqueries to surface trends by platform, region, and genre.
projects.result: Identified high-performing titles and emerging market trends and demonstrated the full data modeling lifecycle from raw CSV to analytical query.
highlights: Normalized 6-table MySQL schema: Games, Platforms, Publishers, Regions, Year, Sales
Cleaned ~2,000 raw CSV records with deduplication, null handling, and type normalization
Analytical queries surface genre trends, regional market share, and platform performance using joins, aggregations, and subqueries

projects.name: iConsultancy: Nonprofit Data Analysis
projects.problem: A nonprofit had inconsistent, uncleaned donor and pickup-request data with no framework for data-driven operational decisions.
projects.evidence: Cleaned and standardized multi-year donor and pickup datasets, built donor segmentation analysis, created user journey maps from stakeholder interviews, identified data governance and privacy gaps, and delivered dashboards and presentations to leadership.
projects.result: Translated findings into actionable service workflow improvements that directly supported strategic planning decisions.
highlights: Cleaned and standardized multi-year donor and pickup-request datasets
Donor segmentation analysis by giving frequency, recency, and amount
User journey maps built from direct stakeholder interviews
Identified data governance and PII handling gaps in existing workflows

projects.name: Threat Intelligence Briefs
projects.problem: Security teams need concise, actionable threat intelligence as raw CVE advisories are too dense to act on quickly.
projects.evidence: Authored 10+ threat briefs analyzing real-world CVE incidents, mapped adversary TTPs to the MITRE ATT&CK framework, and outlined mitigations aligned with NIST and federal guidance for each incident.
projects.result: Produced structured, decision-ready intelligence reports covering the full incident-to-mitigation chain across a range of CVEs and threat actors.
highlights: 10+ reports spanning CVEs across multiple threat actor categories
Each brief follows: incident summary, TTP mapping to MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs, NIST 800-53 mitigation chain
Mitigations cross-referenced with NIST 800-53 controls and federal guidance for each incident

projects.name: Internet Systems Architecture & Simulation
projects.problem: Understanding secure network architecture requires hands-on configuration experience across all layers that theory alone cannot provide.
projects.evidence: Simulated and analyzed a full internet architecture in Cisco Packet Tracer, reconfiguring IP addressing, DHCP, NAT, and routing tables with custom IPv4/IPv6 subnets verified through pings and traceroutes. Set up DNS and HTTP servers for custom domain entries and validated application-layer services from client browsers. Established VPN tunnels between networks with encrypted communication validation, edited IoT and API server behavior, and traced RIP, RIPng, and IoT packets while monitoring the SDN dashboard for host and router provisioning.
projects.result: Demonstrated end-to-end network design and analysis from Layer 2 to the application layer, covering IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack, VPN security, IoT integration, and SDN monitoring across a simulated enterprise topology.
highlights: Reconfigured IP addressing, DHCP, NAT, and routing tables with custom IPv4/IPv6 subnets, verified through pings and traceroutes
Set up DNS and HTTP servers for custom domain entries and validated application-layer services from client browsers
Established VPN tunnels between networks and validated end-to-end encrypted communication
Edited IoT and API server behavior within the simulation environment
Traced RIP, RIPng, and IoT packets using Cisco Packet Tracer simulation mode
Monitored SDN dashboard for host and router provisioning across the topology

projects.name: USG iSchool Chatbot
projects.problem: An in-progress university chatbot lacked alignment between stakeholder expectations, student needs, and actual response quality.
projects.evidence: Joined mid-project and partnered with advisors, IT, and departmental stakeholders to define success criteria; analyzed the RAG-based LLM architecture to improve context structuring; and moderated quality using like/dislike feedback signals to refine response guidelines.
projects.result: Improved chatbot accuracy and relevance through structured feedback loops and better context management aligned with real student-facing use cases.
highlights: Joined mid-project on an active university RAG-based chatbot deployment
Mapped requirements across advisors, IT, and departmental stakeholders
Identified context structuring improvements in the RAG pipeline to improve response relevance
Implemented like/dislike feedback collection for iterative quality refinement

projects.name: Smart Pet Litter Box Redesign
projects.problem: Existing smart litter box products had persistent usability and maintenance pain points that user research confirmed competitors had not adequately addressed.
projects.evidence: Led end-to-end product development by identifying pain points through user research and competitive analysis, defining problem statements and user personas, and translating insights into prioritized requirements aligned with user values and feasibility. Created low-fidelity prototypes targeting cleaning workflows, odor control, and user interactions, and built a phased product roadmap with documented tradeoffs.
projects.result: Delivered a prioritized feature set and prototype grounded in user research, with a phased roadmap outlining planned improvements and their tradeoffs.
highlights: Identified usability and maintenance pain points through user research and competitive analysis of existing products
Defined problem statements and user personas by translating research insights into requirements aligned with user values and feasibility
Created low-fidelity prototypes for cleaning workflows, odor control, and user interaction patterns
Documented feature tradeoffs and outlined a phased product roadmap for future improvements
